title: Cheilanthes bonapartei
intro: Cheilanthes bonapartei, also known as the Bonaparte's lipfern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cheilanthes bonapartei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal effects. In New Zealand, the plant is often made into teas or infusions, as well as being used in tinctures and topical preparations, to harness its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Cheilanthes bonapartei is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Cheilanthes, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the lip ferns. The species is native to South Africa and has distinctive fronds with a leathery texture. The classification of Cheilanthes bonapartei is further divided into various ranks, including kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other ferns in the Pteridaceae family.

parts: Cheilanthes bonapartei, also known as the Bonaparte's lip fern or Bonaparte's fern, is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine.
